rewrites.newDefinition: Hours after sunset spent socializing, dining, or unwinding with company.
Curving the lips upward to express warmth, pleasure, or quiet welcome.
Restaurant staff member taking orders and bringing food to tables.
Vibration in the air registered by the ear as music or noise.
Pronged utensil used for spearing pasta and lifting bites to mouth.
Planned undertaking with a clear goal and steps toward completion.
Forming letters and words on paper or screen to record meaning.
Paying focused attention to sounds, voices, or stories around you.
Enclosed heated chamber used for baking pizza, bread, or roasted dishes.
Person traveling for pleasure, exploring places far from home.
Preparing food by applying heat until ready to eat.
Friendly upward curve of the lips, signaling warmth or amusement.
Stretch of evening hours spent working, dining, or relaxing socially.
Consuming food, often as part of a shared meal.
Glowing display surface where text, images, or news appear.
Matched sound endings; loosely, the rhythmic coherence of a story.
Bristled cleaning tool, mythologized in folklore as a witch's vehicle.
Fabricated narrative or device, here built to justify persecution.
Aching unfulfillable longing central to German Romantic literary tradition.
Light persistent drizzle, common on German peaks during cool spring.
Ordinary working weekday outside Sundays and public holidays.
Mental recollection of something experienced or witnessed in the past.
Speaking softly under one's breath, barely above a whisper.
Drawing air into the lungs and releasing it again steadily.
Solemn ceremony for burying or honoring someone who has died.
Causing pain in body or heart; making something ache.
Upper torso between neck and stomach, where strong emotions register.
Delayed arrival or departure, especially of trains, flights, or appointments.
Chosen path among options, marking commitment to one possibility.
Picking up on something through perception, memory, or close attention.
Producing a high tone with the mouth or wind through gaps.
Pulling apart of two sides, whether political border or human relationship.
